original_hypothesis.title: WebAuthn requireUV Hardcoded to False Reduces Passkey Security to User Presence Only
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: CoinbaseSmartWallet._validateSignature hardcodes requireUV: false when calling WebAuthn.verify. This means the User Verified flag in authenticatorData is never checked, allowing signatures from authenticators that performed only user presence verification (physical tap) without user verification (biometric/PIN). This reduces the security of passkey-owned wallets from two-factor to single-factor for physical access scenarios.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: With requireUV=false, an attacker with physical access to an unlocked device containing the passkey can authorize any wallet operation with just a tap, without biometric or PIN verification. For wallets controlling significant funds, this is a meaningful reduction in security guarantees.

proof_of_signal.pattern: WebAuthn.verify({challenge: ..., requireUV: false, ...}) — hardcoded false for user verification requirement

preconditions: Attacker has physical access to device with the passkey
Device allows UP-only assertion (no UV required)

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: Hardcoded requireUV: false bypasses user verification enforcement
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: UV flag check in WebAuthn.sol lines 146-148 is gated by requireUV parameter which is always false
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: No configurable requireUV setting, no per-operation UV enforcement

analysis_questions: Which authenticator platforms perform UV vs UP-only in different contexts?
Could this be made configurable per-owner or per-operation type?
What is the practical attack surface for UP-only assertions?
validation_requirements: Test with authenticator that supports UP-only assertions
Verify that UV flag is not checked in current implementation
Assess impact of UV enforcement on common authenticator flows

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Attacker with momentary physical access to victim's unlocked phone triggers a passkey assertion with just a tap (UP=1, UV=0). The resulting signature is valid because requireUV=false. Attacker uses this to sign a UserOp transferring all wallet funds to their address.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Complete wallet fund theft through single physical interaction
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: None if attacker completes fund transfer before detection

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No test differentiating UV=0 vs UV=1 authenticator behavior

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
